India is passing through the phase of economic transition and while the problem of undernutrition continues to be a major problem, prevalence of overnutrition is emerging as a significant problem, especially in the urban areas. The prevalence of overweight/ obesity is higher among the women (10.9%) compared to men (7.8%) in 4 8 rural areas . The prevalence of Diabetes Mellitus and Coronary Heart Disease 9(CHD) is also higher in urban areas as compared to their rural counterparts.
